text:
Centre for Epilepsy

Neurosciences Division 

Clinic Date: 20 May 2024 

Dr Aisha Rahman 
Consultant Neurologist  
Centre for Epilepsy  
King’s College Hospital, Denmark Hill, London SE5 9RS  

Dear Dr Aisha Rahman, 

Mr Thomas Green, DOB: 22-09-1987, Hospital No: K7845129 NHS No. 9432175068  
Flat 12, 45 Station Parade, London, NW5 4HT  

Diagnosis: 

Recurrent unprovoked seizures of uncertain classification; current working diagnosis remains under review pending further corroborative data.

Aetiology: 

No definitive structural, genetic, or metabolic cause identified to date. Brain MRI (March 2024) reported normal. Routine EEG (February 2024) non-specific without clear epileptiform discharges.

Other Medical Problems: 

Mild intermittent low back pain attributed to prolonged standing at work; seasonal allergic rhinitis managed with as-needed non-sedating antihistamine.

Management Plan: 

Continue Lamotrigine 100 mg twice daily. Reinforce adherence and single-brand prescribing to minimise variability. Arrange extended ambulatory EEG with video (72-hour) to capture habitual events. Bloods today: FBC, U&E, LFT, Lamotrigine trough level. Provide updated workplace letter outlining safety considerations around crowd surges, heat stress, and sleep disruption. Review in four months; sooner if breakthrough seizures occur or adverse effects emerge.

Since our last appointment, the patient and family report improved overall control. The family feel seizure frequency has decreased markedly since starting medication, with only five seizures so far this year. Events remain brief (30–60 seconds) with rapid recovery, typically preceded by a sense of internal "rush" and occasional lip-smacking noted by relatives. No clear precipitants, though episodes have clustered after consecutive early shifts and during exceptionally busy gate-line periods with crowd surges. No injuries reported. He has self-restricted lone ladder use and heavy manual coupling tasks during early mornings.

We discussed occupational risk in the context of his role at a busy metro station gate line, where prolonged standing, fluctuating temperatures, and crowd dynamics can be fatiguing. I have advised practical measures: maintaining regular breaks where service permits, hydration, avoidance of missed meals, prioritising consistent sleep, and avoiding lone work in high-risk areas. He will liaise with his manager regarding rota patterns to limit consecutive early starts and to ensure a colleague is present during peak crowd surges. DVLA guidance has been reviewed; he confirms ongoing compliance and will notify us immediately of any change in frequency or severity.

He denies rash, mood change, or notable cognitive side effects on Lamotrigine. Occasional dizziness reported when dehydrated on hot platforms; we discussed countermeasures. No current plans for dose escalation given improved control and tolerability; future adjustments will be guided by seizure diary, levels, and ambulatory EEG findings.

Yours sincerely

entities.text: with only five seizures so far this year
The family feel seizure frequency has decreased markedly since starting medication, with only five seizures so far this year

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: Extracted explicit statement: "only five seizures so far this year." Clinic date is 20 May 2024, so the observation window is from 1 Jan 2024 to 20 May 2024, approximately 5 months. Therefore, normalize the frequency to 5 per 5 month. No other higher or conflicting frequency mentioned. Not seizure free.
seizure_frequency_number: with only five seizures so far this year
reference: The family feel seizure frequency has decreased markedly since starting medication, with only five seizures so far this year
